title: Toward the Fading Twilight
description: Shimamura finishes work early and walks home through the sunset. Yashiro appears beside her in a boar mascot suit, sharing orange juice and a quiet moment before they part ways.
content:
Finishing the day's work before the sky turned dark brought a small sense of fulfillment. And with tomorrow being a day off, I felt even lighter. Of course, it would be ideal if every day ended like this… though that rarely happens.

Lately I've been thinking: Maybe having more things that don't go smoothly… is just part of becoming an adult.

I am already an adult, though. I laughed wryly to myself and started packing up.

I politely refused a coworker's invitation and headed out. The sunset outside the building dyed the town in fiery colors, and walking through it filled me with a strange loneliness. Maybe it's some ancient instinct passed down from our ancestors—telling us to hurry home before night falls.

Swaying with the train, I planned to stop by the convenience store before going home. With that thought, my steps felt lighter as I climbed the stairs out of the station. The approaching day off made my heart flutter a little. If only this feeling could last forever, I thought now and then.

Just as I reached the top of the subway stairs—

'Turned into… sugar-like color.'

'Whoa!'

A familiar voice popped up from the side. A small, water-colored creature was mixed among the crowd. No, not a child—more like… a boar?

Yashiro was wearing her boar mascot suit.

She'd appeared like this suddenly more than once before, so I wasn't particularly surprised. She looked up at the sugar-colored sky. The sun hung farther on the horizon than it had from the office view. The colors softened, almost dreamlike—so gentle that I could look directly at them without lowering my head. But stare too long, and the image might imprint on my eyes.

'It looks delicious.'

'You say that every time…'

'Fufufu.'

Every time I happened to think about going to the convenience store, she somehow appeared.

'…Are you following me?'

'What?'

Well, whatever. I brought her along and entered the convenience store.

'Do you want something to drink?'

'Hmm… orange juice.'

'Alright, I'll get that too.'

I bought two bottles of orange juice and handed one to her outside.

'Thank you.'

'You even know to say thanks before drinking. That's good.'

We walked home drinking our juice. The little boar walked on two legs, looking tiny and cute.

'Want to go eat dinner?'

'Fufufu, thank you for the invitation, but no need today.'

'That's unexpected.'

I didn't expect her to even have the concept of 'no need to bother.' If her goal wasn't dinner, then… what?

'So, did you need something?'

At my question, the boar's ears flapped cheerfully.

'Fufufu, I simply wanted to walk with Shimamura under the sunset.'

'Oh…?'

'I believe that is all.'

In Yashiro's universe-filled eyes, I felt she was looking at something even farther than the sunset.

'But it really does look like curry.'

'Mhm. Call it whatever you like, see it however you want. Because what you see belongs to you.'

Just like how what Adachi sees is different from what I or anyone else sees.

For Yashiro, whether it's sugar or curry or something else entirely—she could see anything in the sky.

'Mhmm, because curry is delicious.'

Her reasoning never quite connected.

'…………………………'

And so, the two of us walked down the familiar road home.

For some reason, I felt an odd wave of emotion. A nostalgic feeling stirred by something that wasn't nostalgic at all. Something contradictory, yet moving.

Our shadows stretched longer and longer in the twilight, swallowed as the dusk faded like it was being wiped away by nightfall. Just as our shadows disappeared, Yashiro stopped and looked up at me.

'The sunset has gone, so we part here. It is time for dinner.'

'Alright then… say hello to everyone for me.'

My little sister, Father, and Mother. She knew them well by now.

'Just say hello, that'll be enough.'

'Okay.'

Carefully holding the half-finished bottle of juice, Yashiro ran off. I wondered if her home was really in that direction as I watched her leave.

Then I turned forward again.

'Oh.'

I spotted the silhouette of my housemate in the distance. I narrowed my eyes—the shirt on her back seemed to shift colors, dyed by the last scraps of sunset.

Adachi was a little far ahead, and of course she hadn't noticed me. That was normal… yet I felt a small disappointment. Though if it were Adachi, maybe…

'…Actually, this kind of thing happens with her a lot.'

Every time we part, I seem to run into Adachi right away. Is she guiding me? If so… I can't help but smile.

Then I took long strides toward her. I wanted to catch up before the night arrived completely.

Finishing work early really does let good things happen.
